prerequisites: A Smartsheet account on a Business or Enterprise plan, which is required to generate API access tokens
The ID of the sheet (or report) you want to sync, supplied via the spreadsheet_id config field. Set is_report to true when the ID refers to a report rather than a sheet
An access token (raw API token, or an OAuth 2.0 token obtained for your registered app) authorized for the target sheet or report
Optionally, start_datetime to bound the sync to rows created or modified on or after a given time, and metadata_fields to select which sheet metadata columns to include

authMethods.name: API access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Provide a raw Smartsheet API access token in credentials.access_token. Every request is authenticated by sending it in the HTTP header as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Generate the token in the Smartsheet UI under Account (profile image) > Personal Settings > API Access > Generate new access token. Token generation requires a Business or Enterprise plan.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Use Smartsheet's OAuth 2.0 authorization flow with a registered developer app. Register the app in Developer Tools to obtain the App client id (credentials.client_id) and App secret (credentials.client_secret), redirect the user to Smartsheet's authorization endpoint to receive an authorization code, then exchange that code at POST /token with grant_type=authorization_code for an access_token (credentials.access_token) and refresh_token (credentials.refresh_token). When the access token expires, request a new pair by calling POST /token with grant_type=refresh_token using the stored refresh token.

setupSteps.title: Confirm your Smartsheet plan and identify the sheet
setupSteps.body: Generating API access tokens requires a Business or Enterprise plan. Identify the sheet or report you want to sync and note its ID for the spreadsheet_id config field. If the ID belongs to a report rather than a sheet, plan to set is_report to true.

setupSteps.title: Obtain credentials (raw token or OAuth)
setupSteps.body: For a raw token, open the Smartsheet UI, go to Account (profile image) > Personal Settings > API Access, and choose Generate new access token. For OAuth 2.0, register a developer app in Developer Tools to get its App client id and App secret, run the authorization flow to capture an authorization code, and exchange the code for an access token and refresh token.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Populate the credentials field. For token auth, set credentials.access_token to the generated token. For OAuth, set credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token from your app registration and token exchange. Requests are sent with the Authorization: Bearer header.

setupSteps.title: Configure the sync fields
setupSteps.body: Set the required spreadsheet_id to the target sheet or report ID. Set is_report when the ID is a report. Optionally set start_datetime to limit rows to those at or after a timestamp, and metadata_fields to include specific sheet metadata in the output.

sources.title: Smartsheet API Authentication and Access Tokens
